responses.content: and generally dirty and gritty conditions inside a steel mill. When Margaret finally got permission, the technical problems began. Black-and-white film at that time was sensitive to blue light, not the reds and oranges of hot steel. The pictures came out all black. Margaret solved this problem by bringing along a new style of flare (which produces white light) and having assistants hold them to light her scenes. Her abilities resulted in some of the best steel factory pictures of that era, and these earned her national attention.
responses.bucket_rationale: The student's most recent highlight is far too long and includes multiple sentences, exceeding the character limit for an acceptable answer. The question asks for a specific sentence or phrase that describes the problem Margaret encountered or how she solved it. The student's highlight includes both the problem and the solution, as well as additional context, making it much longer than any acceptable answer. Therefore, it is marked as incorrect due to excessive length.
